prakriti_types.description: Predominantly Sattvika individuals are characterized by clarity, wisdom, and inner harmony. They have a natural inclination towards truth, compassion, and selfless service.
strengths: Strong moral compass and ethical foundation
Natural leadership through wisdom and example
Deep empathy and compassion for all beings
growth_areas: May be overly idealistic, needing to balance with practical reality
Could benefit from assertiveness in certain situations
Risk of spiritual bypassing or avoiding necessary conflict
daily_practices: Morning meditation (20 minutes) and pranayama
Sattvic diet: fresh fruits, vegetables, whole grains, milk
Regular study of uplifting literature
Practice of gratitude journaling before sleep
Seva (selfless service) in the community

prakriti_types.description: Predominantly Rajasika individuals are driven, ambitious, and energetic. They thrive on activity, achievement, and competition, but may struggle with restlessness and impatience.
strengths: High energy and strong drive to achieve goals
Natural ability to lead and motivate others
Resilience in the face of challenges
growth_areas: Developing deeper listening and empathy skills
daily_practices: Cooling pranayama (Sheetali, Chandrabhedana) morning and evening
Moderate exercise followed by relaxation (yoga nidra)
Reduce stimulants (caffeine, spicy food); favour cooling foods
Practice active listening in one conversation daily
Evening journaling to process emotions constructively

prakriti_types.description: Predominantly Tamasika individuals value stability and comfort. They tend towards routine and familiarity, but may need to cultivate more energy, motivation, and engagement.
strengths: Ability to remain unfazed by external chaos
Deep capacity for rest and recovery
growth_areas: Cultivating curiosity and openness to new experiences
daily_practices: Wake before sunrise; avoid daytime sleeping
Energising pranayama (Kapalabhati, Bhastrika) each morning
Light, warm, freshly cooked meals; avoid heavy and processed foods
Set one small achievable goal daily and celebrate completion
Engage in social activity or community involvement weekly

prakriti_types.description: A blend of wisdom and drive. Sattva-Rajasika individuals combine ethical clarity with the ambition to make things happen, making them effective and principled leaders.
strengths: Ability to inspire and uplift teams
Strong moral foundation paired with competitive edge
growth_areas: Managing the tension between idealism and ambition
Avoiding burnout from trying to do too much
daily_practices: Morning meditation (15 min) followed by a brisk walk or yoga
Balance sattvic base diet with moderate rajasic foods for energy
Alternate between achievement-focused and service-focused activities
Practice delegation and trust-building weekly
End day with reflective journaling on impact vs. ego

prakriti_types.description: A combination of inner wisdom and deep inward focus. Sattva-Tamasika individuals are thoughtful and contemplative, though they may need to work on translating insight into action.
strengths: Ability to see beyond surface-level issues
growth_areas: Engaging more actively with the external world
daily_practices: Gentle morning yoga followed by meditation
Light sattvic meals; include warming spices (ginger, turmeric)
One action item daily to translate a thought into reality
Regular walks in nature to maintain grounding and energy
Weekly social engagement to stay connected

prakriti_types.description: High energy tempered with ethical awareness. Rajo-Sattvika individuals are passionate about their goals while maintaining a sense of fairness and concern for others.
strengths: Ability to rally others around a vision
High energy with moments of deep reflection
growth_areas: Learning to slow down and listen more
daily_practices: Morning pranayama (alternate nostril breathing) and brief meditation
Moderate diet balancing energy needs with calming foods
Schedule daily quiet time (15 min) for reflection
Channel competitive energy into collaborative projects
Practice gratitude to counter restless dissatisfaction

prakriti_types.description: A mix of intense drive and deep inertia. Rajo-Tamasika individuals oscillate between bursts of energy and periods of withdrawal, requiring balance and structure.
strengths: Intense focus during periods of motivation
Ability to understand both action and rest
growth_areas: Managing emotional swings between activity and lethargy
Building sustainable energy rather than boom-bust cycles
daily_practices: Fixed wake-up time with light exercise (walking or stretching)
Three regular meals at consistent times; reduce processed foods
Alternate between stimulating and calming activities throughout the day
Practice mindful breathing when feeling either restless or lethargic
Set micro-goals to build consistency and confidence
